# Transcrição: 02 Custom Conversion

- **Fonte:** 02 Custom Conversion

## [00:00 - 03:08]

Okay guys, so what I want to share with you in this training is standard events. Understanding standard events and the role that they play and then how to use them and install them properly. So let's take a look at standard events and why you need to use them. Some of you have may never heard of the term standard events before or some of you might be very comfortable with them already. And on that same note you might be wondering what's the difference between a standard event and a custom conversion. So what I want to show you is here in the pixels tab, you have two options when you're tracking online conversions using the Facebook pixel. I'll just go into the pixel here. So the first option is standard events which are installed into your website's code or you can track using custom conversions which are typically URL based. So the difference is very similar but typically standard events are more robust because they're installed directly into your website's code. So to set up your standard events, you would manually install one of the event codes from here. So if you have an e-commerce website and at any point if you want to run DPAs then you have to install the standard events and so that Facebook can track your website users activity. So there's nine standard events that you can track for and we'll have a look through them quickly now. So the first is search. So tracking people searching for items on your website. You've got view content which is viewing any content on your website. So things like products, you would put a view content tracking code on. Add to cart tracks people adding items to their shopping cart. Add to wishlist. If your website has a wishlist feature then it would be people saving that item to the wishlist. Initiate checkout tracks people who start the checkout process but don't necessarily complete it. Add payment info is one step further than initiate checkout where they're actually going through the process of adding in their payment details or credit card details but again doesn't necessarily mean that they've purchased it. Purchase is the one that a lot of people on here will be tracking for and that's people purchasing products on your website. Lead is if you're running any lead generation campaigns so you could use this for your dentist or if you're tracking newsletter sign ups and then complete registration is any registration form on your page so it could be a subscription to a service or something like that.

## [03:08 - 06:09]

And then there's also an option down here for installing a custom event on your website as well so you can add in your own parameters to track whatever event it is that you're tracking for. So the important thing to note with these standard events is where you put them on your website. So you need to make sure that you're putting them on thank you page of whatever event it is that you're tracking. So for example if you're tracking for leads and you want to track people that sign up to your newsletter you want to put this code here on the page after they click the submit button or the confirm button. So what that's doing and how that works is that it's essentially telling Facebook that when someone visits that website that URL and that code gets fired then that means that they've signed up so it makes it really easy for Facebook to tell exactly what a lead is and then you can attribute that back to your ads if they came from your app. So it's really important that standard events go on the page after the action takes place and it's the same thing with custom conversions as well. If you're using a URL based custom conversion you want that URL to be the thank you URL. So in short these are the nine standard event categories and situations that you'd use them in. Let's take a look at custom conversions as well. So Facebook recently made custom conversions a lot more robust than they used to be. So it used to be only URL based but now you can also put custom conversions for any events that you have standard events that you have installed on your website. So you've got any purchases or leads or anything you can add in standard events for those as well. And you can make custom conversions that are dependent on those actions. But primarily if you don't have access to your client's website and you can't install anything into the code then you may need to use a custom conversion as a URL based custom conversion to track those actions in this way. So for example if you couldn't add in code onto the thank you page for someone signing up to your client's newsletter you'd be able to add in the URL here for that thank you page. And then it would create the conversion for you to track that way as well. And now you actually have the ability to optimize based on these custom conversions as well which is something that you didn't used to be able to do. So that's really handy too. The other thing that you can do is what I was mentioning before where you can set up custom conversions based on events on your website. And the other cool thing is that you can now add in event parameters for those events. So I don't have any purchase events on this on this pixel yet.
